data.sentence: Hi, this is Marcus Williams calling from Aegis Cloud Security support, I have Vanessa Cruz on the line for case number 8749, and I'm also going to bring in my colleague Elena Vasquez who specializes in Identity — Elena are you there?
Yeah I'm here, hi Vanessa, thanks for your patience on this one.

Hi, yeah, okay — look I appreciate you guys getting on a call but I want to be upfront, this has been a really frustrating morning for us, so I'm hoping we can just cut to the chase and figure out what's going on.

Absolutely, totally understand, let's dive right in — so from the case notes it looks like you're seeing an issue with SAML certificate rotation in Aegis Identity, can you walk us through what you're experiencing?

Sure, so — we rotated our SAML signing certificate yesterday, standard procedure, we do this every six months, and after the rotation a significant portion of our users are getting authentication failures when they try to SSO into our internal tools.
Okay and when you say a significant portion, are we talking like a handful of users or is this more widespread?

We're talking roughly sixty to seventy percent of our workforce right now, Elena, so — yeah, it's pretty bad, people can't get into Salesforce, they can't get into our inventory management system, it's basically grinding our retail ops team to a halt on a Sunday which is one of our busiest days.
Okay that's — yeah that's definitely a critical impact situation, I'm sorry you're dealing with that on a Sunday.
I mean, it shouldn't matter what day it is, this shouldn't be happening at all, right? We followed the documentation for certificate rotation step by step and now our people can't log in.

You're right, you're absolutely right, and I want to dig into this because I actually have a hypothesis about what might be happening here — Vanessa, when you did the rotation, did you use the in-platform certificate wizard or did you upload the certificate manually through the admin console?
We used the wizard, the one that's supposed to handle the, uh, the propagation automatically, that's kind of the whole point of using it.

Right, okay, so — I need to be transparent with you here Vanessa, we are aware of a bug that was introduced in the 3.4.1 release of Aegis Identity, and it specifically affects the certificate rotation wizard.
There's a bug.

Yes, and — I want to explain what it's doing because it helps with the workaround — so essentially what's happening is the wizard is completing the cert rotation on the identity provider side but it's not properly propagating the new certificate metadata to all of the service provider connections, so some of your SP integrations are still trying to validate assertions against the old certificate.
So the wizard is broken. The tool that's supposed to make this safe and easy — is broken.
I — yes, there's a defect in it, I'm not going to sugarcoat that, and I understand why that's incredibly frustrating especially given the impact you're seeing right now.

And this is — okay, sorry Marcus, I'm just — I'm really struggling here because we've been on this platform for two and a half years and we've had a rough few weeks, you guys had that Detect outage earlier this month and now this, it's like—
I hear you, I hear you completely and those are both legitimate concerns, but right now let's focus on getting your people back in, can we do that? Because I do have a path to resolution.
Yes, okay, yes please — what do we need to do.

Okay so the fix is — it's a bit manual but it works, what you'll need to do is go into each service provider connection in the admin console and manually re-import the new certificate metadata for each integration, it won't be automatic through the wizard but once you do it per-SP the authentication will work correctly.
How many SP connections are we talking about, like, do you know how many integrations we have configured?
I'm pulling up your account right now — it looks like you have fourteen service provider connections active.
Fourteen. Okay so we have to manually touch fourteen integrations to fix something that your wizard was supposed to handle automatically.
Yes, and — I can stay on this call with you the entire time and walk you through each one if that's helpful, I don't want you to have to figure this out alone.
I'm going to need to pull in one of my engineers to actually do the clicking around in the console, give me two minutes.
Of course, take your time.
While we wait — Elena, do you know when a proper fix is going to be released for this? Like when is the patch coming?
So our engineering team has the fix built, it's going through QA validation right now, current target is a patch release in the 3.4.2 update which is scheduled for the week of April 6th.
April 6th. So for anyone who does a cert rotation before then they're going to hit this same problem.

That's correct, and we are — we have a customer advisory going out today actually, warning admins not to use the wizard until 3.4.2, I know that doesn't help you retroactively but we are trying to prevent other customers from hitting this.
Would have been nice to have that warning before I ran the wizard yesterday.
You're right, the advisory should have gone out sooner, I — I don't have a good answer for why it didn't and I'm not going to make excuses for that.
Okay, I have my engineer Priya on — she can go into the console, Elena can you walk us through the steps?
Yes absolutely — Priya, if you go to the Aegis Identity admin console and navigate to Identity Federation, then Service Providers, you should see the list of all your SP connections there.

And Vanessa, just so you know, Marcus and I are going to document everything from this call and I'm going to personally escalate this to our customer success team to make sure you get a proper follow-up on the overall reliability concerns you raised, the Detect outage, this bug — I want to make sure someone is having that bigger conversation with you.
I appreciate that, I do — I just, I need things to actually work, you know, we chose Aegis because we thought it was going to reduce our operational headaches not add to them.
That's fair, and that feedback is going to be heard, I promise you that — now let's get your team back online.
